required_protocol_sentence: DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
assignment: Discovery only in isolated scratch. Replace the rejected named async-wrapper attribution with one predeclared full active-window allocation method that includes allocations across async continuation boundaries. Use separate fresh processes and inspector sampling with collected objects included. For each DKR-P5 row, freeze a zero-operation control, one-batch observation, and two-batch observation before measurement. Define the zero subtraction, bytes-per-completed-operation calculation, minimum signal-to-control ratio, and acceptable corrected two-to-one proportionality band before running. Preserve exact operation counts, cleanup ownership, post-close zero live resources, and session post-close mutation rejection. Then, only if calibration passes for all three rows, run one frozen exact-head A/A allocation comparison in an interleaved order and require every row to stay inside a predeclared noise band. Do not reuse or widen the rejected DKR-P6 wrapper bands. Do not rerun until favorable. Do not edit product or .okra and do not claim baseline/candidate improvement or CKR success.

budget_and_stop_rule.stop: Run the new zero/one/two calibration and conditional A/A comparison once, accept or reject the method, and stop.

output_schema: Executable checkpoint with predeclaration chronology/hash, exact runtime/build, scripts/hashes, zero/one/two raw bytes and corrected arithmetic per row, conditional A/A results, cleanup walls, replay, and reducing decision. Worker evidence is not acceptance.
